commands.review_summary:
The PR adds guarded copying/projection for Plugin SDK provider auth/env arrays and provider catalog rows/models, plus focused tests for throwing getters and revoked proxies.

PR surface: Source +264, Tests +474. Total +738 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main uses direct array/object projection paths that can throw on unreadable plugin-owned rows, and the PR adds focused proxy/getter tests that exercise those source paths; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This maintainer-owned draft has no narrow automated repair; the next action is maintainer review/landing decision plus normal CI or waiver for the provider/auth compatibility risk.

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): isolate provider catalog projection failures
target.body:
## Summary

- splits the critical provider/catalog slice out of the larger fuzz quarry branch at https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87141
- hardens Plugin SDK provider entry projection so unreadable auth/env rows skip only the bad row while preserving healthy `mockplugin` auth methods and env vars
- hardens provider catalog projection so unreadable or revoked `fuzzplugin` provider/model rows do not erase healthy sibling model catalog rows
- keeps the deferred install catalog, provider-auth choice, setup registry, UI/status, and Codex app-server fuzz work out of this PR

## Verification

- final refresh on `origin/main` `5660b6706230d761e0f78bb267a082a0f774cf46`: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 2 files passed, 22 tests passed at `8a7ab79652857b2ff650f0e0d1d9648d471cd596`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog-unified-text.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SKIP_PREPARE=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog-unified-text.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- branch diff and spec scrubbed for the reported plugin name and private local paths: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean on base `55d4456751b9952ded31538126acdb63764d3989`; after the final rebase to `5660b6706230d761e0f78bb267a082a0f774cf46`, the focused proof above remained green
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: blocked before remote execution because Blacksmith is not authenticated (`blacksmith auth login` required)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: provider SDK/provider catalog projection no longer lets one bad plugin-owned provider/auth/model row poison healthy sibling provider catalog or auth rows.

Real environment tested: local linked OpenClaw worktree `fuzz-critical-provider-catalog-20260530` rebased on `origin/main` `5660b6706230d761e0f78bb267a082a0f774cf46`, head `8a7ab79652857b2ff650f0e0d1d9648d471cd596`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest for `src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts` and `src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts`, formatter, targeted oxlint, diff check, private-name scrub, final-base autoreview, and attempted Blacksmith Testbox changed gate.

Evidence after fix: tests now cover unreadable provider catalog maps, unreadable provider entries, unreadable provider model rows, revoked provider/model proxy rows, auth arrays with broken array methods, unreadable auth array slots, unreadable auth option rows, and paired provider catalog rows while proving healthy `mockplugin` siblings survive.

Observed result after fix: healthy provider model rows still project into the unified text catalog when adjacent `fuzzplugin` rows throw or are revoked, and healthy auth/env entries still register when adjacent provider auth slots are unreadable.

What was not tested: broad `pnpm check:changed` did not run because the maintainer Testbox path is blocked by missing Blacksmith authentication in this shell. AWS fallback was not run for this small split after the focused proof and final-base autoreview passed.
target.author: vincentkoc
